e. Wilson, agent of one of the local
labor unions, made complaint to the
mayor this afternoon that Contractor
Costello was working his men on pub
lie work nine hours per day, CON
trary to the provisions of the eight
hour law, which has lately been de-
clared constitutional by the supreme
court.


Wilson claimed he had been unable
to secure an injunction restraining
Costello from working the men nine
hours because the remittitur had not
been received here showing officially
just what the supreme court had de-
cided.


Mayor Boyd sent for Corporation
Counsel Dudley, who gaveit as his
opinion that the work could be
stopped at once without waiting foi
the remittitur as the decision be-
came operative as soon as made. He
